title: cerastium fischerianum subsp. albimarginatum
intro: Cerastium fischerianum subsp. albimarginatum, also known as white-fringed mouse-ear or white-fringed cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pink family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Cerastium fischerianum subsp. albimarginatum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, as well as in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although its use should be approached with caution due to limited scientific evidence and potential interactions with other medications.
